Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q500763]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q500763]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Other places|Kibitsu jinja (disambiguation)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kibitsuhiko Jinja
吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|WD=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|WD=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|WD=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|WD=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Other places|Kibitsu jinja (disambiguation)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kibitsuhiko Jinja
吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo|1=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|WD=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan|1=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|WD=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie|1=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|1=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]][[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Other places|Kibitsu jinja (disambiguation)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kibitsuhiko Jinja
吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo|1=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|WD=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan|1=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|WD=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie|1=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|1=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo|1=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|WD=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan|1=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|WD=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie|1=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|1=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|WD=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|WD=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|qid=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan|1=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|qid=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie|1=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|qid=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|qid=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|qid=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|qid=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|1=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|qid=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|qid=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} [[fr:Kibitsuhiko-jinja]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[zh:吉備津彥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500763}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Okayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Other places|Kibitsu jinja (disambiguation)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kibitsuhiko Jinja
吉備津彦神社 | image = File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja tourou and haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden and Stone lantern of Kibitsuhiko Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.67672222|N|133.86386111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kibitsuhiko Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qid=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1043 Ichinomiya, Kita-ku, Okayama-shi, Okayama-ken 701-1211 | website = {{official|1=https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = third weekend of October | leadership = | footnotes = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsuhiko Jinja'''|吉備津彦神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|qid=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the third weekend of October. Theshrine is also known in literature as the {{nihongo|Asahi-no-miya|朝日の宮||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Kibitsuhiko Jinja are:{{fact|date=November 2022}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]]''|吉備津彦命|}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} and conqueror of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]] * {{nihongo|'''Wakatakehiko-no-mikoto'''| 稚武彦命|}}, younger brother or son of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}'''| 孝霊天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}'''|孝元天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}'''| 開化天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'''| 崇神天皇 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Hikosashikatawake-no-mikoto'''| 彦刺肩別命 |}}, brother of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Amatarashi-hiko Kunioshi-hito no mikoto'''|天足彦国押人命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''Yamato-totohimo-moso-hime-no-mikoto'''| 倭迹迹日百襲姫命 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamato-totohiwakaya-hime-no-mikoto'''|大倭迹々日稚屋比売命 |}}, younger sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Kanayamahiko-no-kami'''|金山彦大神 |}}, elder sister of Kibitsuhiko * {{nihongo|'''Ōyamakuni-no-kami'''|大山咋神 |}} ==History== The origins of Kibitsuhiko Jinja are uncertain. According to the shrine's legend', it was during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}, and was the site of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto's residence; but the shrine does not appear in any historical documentation until the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is not listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' and instead [[Ani Shrine]] was given the Rank of {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社||}} and the Rank of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. However, Ani Jinja sided with {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|qid=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo|1=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}} in his revolt from 939 to 941 and was thus demoted. On the other hand, [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]], the parent shrine of the Kibitsuhiko Jinja wrote prayers for the victory of imperial forces over Fujiwara no Sumitomo, and was thus rewarded by having their branch shrine in Bizen raised to become the ''ichinomiya''. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was burned down by the {{ill|Matsuda clan|en|Matsuda clan|qid=Q2975315|lt=Matsuda clan|1=Matsuda clan}}, but was restored with the support of {{ill|Ukita Naoie|en|Ukita Naoie|qid=Q1045148|lt=Ukita Naoie|1=Ukita Naoie}}. {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here for victory prior to his attack on [[Takamatsu Castle (Bitchū)|Takamatsu Castle]]. In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], [[Ikeda Toshitaka]], the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|qid=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} and the lord of {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|qid=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}, rebuilt the shrine, and successive rulers of {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|qid=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} sponsored various repairs and reconstructions. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was listed as a Prefecture Shrine, and was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} in 1928.In December 1930, the main shrine and most other structures (with the exception of the Zuijinmon Gate) burned down due to accident. The shrine buildings that can be seen today were completed in 1936.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is a three-minute walk from {{ill|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|en|Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|qid=Q4919389|lt=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station|1=Bizen-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|qid=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== *[[Tachi]] Japanese sword. [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Signed by Ikeda Shinkai and dated 1678, this sword was donation to the shrine by {{ill|Ikeda Tsunamasa|en|Ikeda Tsunamasa|qid=Q3148404|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}}, It is now kept at the Okayama Prefectural Museum.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6336 |title=太刀〈銘（菊紋）井上真改／延宝五年八月日〉|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=20 August 2020}} ==Gallery== Kibitsuhiko jinja -01.jpg|Panorama File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja honden.JPG|Honden（Okayama P.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin-mon（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko Jinja 07.JPG|Stone tōrō lantern（Okayama C.ICP） File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja Heian-sugi.JPG|Peace Sugi File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja torii.JPG|Torii File:Kibitsuhiko-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} *[https://www.kibitsuhiko.or.jp Official home page] {{Kibi clan}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{Commons category|Kibitsuhiko-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q500763|co|commons:Category:Kibitsuhiko-jinja|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|fr|Kibitsuhiko-jinja|ja|吉備津彦神社|zh|吉備津彥神社}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
